programme.title: Tombstone
programme.desc: After success cleaning up Dodge City, Wyatt Earp moves to Tombstone, Arizona, and wishes to get rich in obscurity. He meets his brothers there, as well as his old friend Doc Holliday. A band of outlaws that call themselves The Cowboys are causing problems in the region with various acts of random violence, and inevitably come into confrontation with Holliday and the Earps, which leads to a shoot-out at the O.K. Corral.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Raiders of the Lost Ark
programme.desc: When Dr. Indiana Jones – the tweed-suited professor who just happens to be a celebrated archaeologist – is hired by the government to locate the legendary Ark of the Covenant, he finds himself up against the entire Nazi regime.

programme.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
programme.desc: An apocalyptic story set in the furthest reaches of our planet, in a stark desert landscape where humanity is broken, and most everyone is crazed fighting for the necessities of life. Within this world exist two rebels on the run who just might be able to restore order.

programme.title: Mad Max
programme.desc: Taking place in a dystopian Australia in the near future, Mad Max tells the story of a highway patrolman cruising the squalid back roads that have become the breeding ground of criminals foraging for gasoline and scraps. After some grisly events at the hands of a motorcycle gang, Max sets out across the barren wastelands in search of revenge.

programme.title: Mechanic: Resurrection
programme.desc: Arthur Bishop thought he had put his murderous past behind him when his most formidable foe kidnaps the love of his life. Now he is forced to travel the globe to complete three impossible assassinations, and do what he does best, make them look like accidents.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
programme.desc: An art collector appeals to Indiana Jones to embark on a search for the Holy Grail. He learns that another archaeologist has disappeared while searching for the precious goblet, and the missing man is his own father, Dr. Henry Jones. The artifact is much harder to find than they expected, and its powers are too much for those impure of heart.

programme.title: Baby Driver
programme.desc: Coerced into working for a crime boss, a young getaway driver must face the music when a doomed heist threatens his life, love, and freedom.

programme.title: Constantine
programme.desc: John Constantine has literally been to Hell and back. When he teams up with a policewoman to solve the mysterious suicide of her twin sister, their investigation takes them through the world of demons and angels that exists beneath the landscape of contemporary Los Angeles.

programme.title: The Punisher
programme.desc: After Frank Castle's whole family is gunned down in cold blood, he shall become the vengeful Angel of Death known as the Great Equaliser of Crime: The Punisher. The one-man hurricane of blood and bullets is about to make the thugs who destroyed his life rue the day they made The Punisher come to life.

programme.title: Universal Soldier
programme.desc: An elite team of soldiers has been used against terrorists where they use astounding physical capabilities to overcome them. Victoria, a reporter, follows them and discovers a part of their secret. When one of the team kills her cameraman she tries to escape. Luc, one of the soldiers begins to have flashbacks and turns sides and helps her as the remainder of the team follows to protect their secrets.

programme.title: Mad Max
programme.desc: Taking place in a dystopian Australia in the near future, Mad Max tells the story of a highway patrolman cruising the squalid back roads that have become the breeding ground of criminals foraging for gasoline and scraps. After some grisly events at the hands of a motorcycle gang, Max sets out across the barren wastelands in search of revenge.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Detective Sherlock Holmes and his stalwart partner Watson engage in a battle of wits and brawn with a nemesis whose plot is a threat to all of England.

programme.title: Total Recall
programme.desc: Douglas Quaid is haunted by a recurring dream about a journey to Mars. He hopes to find out more about this dream and buys a holiday at Rekall Inc. where they sell implanted memories. But something goes wrong with the memory implantation and he remembers being a secret agent fighting against the evil Mars administrator Cohaagen. Now the story really begins and it's a rollercoaster ride until the massive end of the movie.

programme.title: The Terminator
programme.desc: In the post-apocalyptic future, reigning tyrannical supercomputers teleport a cyborg assassin known as the "Terminator" back to 1984 to kill Sarah Connor, whose unborn son is destined to lead insurgents against 21st century mechanical hegemony. Meanwhile, the human-resistance movement dispatches a lone warrior to safeguard Sarah. Can he stop the virtually indestructible killing machine?

programme.title: Creature from the Black Lagoon
programme.desc: A scientific expedition searching for fossils along the Amazon River discovers a prehistoric Gill-Man in the legendary Black Lagoon. The explorers capture the mysterious creature, but it breaks free. The Gill-Man returns to kidnap the lovely Kay, fiancée of one in the expedition, with whom he has fallen in love.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Five grisly tales from a kid's comic book about a murdered father rising from his grave, a bizarre meteor, a vengeful husband, a mysterious crate's occupant, and a plague of cockroaches.

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Transylvanian vampire Count Dracula bends a naive real estate agent to his will, then takes up residence at a London estate where he sleeps in his coffin by day and searches for potential victims by night.

programme.title: Ed Wood
programme.desc: Ambitious but troubled movie director Edward D. Wood Jr. tries his best to fulfill his dreams despite his lack of talent.

programme.title: Frankenstein
programme.desc: The 1931 horror movie "Frankenstein", tells the tale of when Henry Frankenstein who is a doctor, trying to discover a way to make a living human using human body parts from the recently deceased. He succeeds, but his creation is looked upon as a monster by the local inhabitants, who wish to destroy it.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Spacemonster
programme.desc: When an atomic war on Mars destroys the planet's women, it's up to Martian Princess Marcuzan and her right-hand man Dr. Nadir to travel to earth and kidnap women for new breeding stock. Landing in Puerto Rico, they shoot down a NASA space capsule manned by an android. With his electronic brain damaged, the android terrorizes the island while the Martians raid beaches and pool parties.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Wolf Man
programme.desc: The resurrected Wolf Man, seeking a cure for his malady, enlists the aid of a mad scientist, who claims he will not only rid the Wolf Man of his nocturnal metamorphosis, but also revive the frozen body of Frankenstein's inhuman creation.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: House of Dracula
programme.desc: A scientist working on cures for rare afflictions, such as a bone softening agent made from molds to allow him to correct the spinal deformity of his nurse, finds the physical causes of lycanthropy in wolf-man Larry Talbot and of vampirism in Count Dracula, but himself becomes afflicted with homicidal madness while exchanging blood with Dracula.

programme.title: House of Frankenstein
programme.desc: After escaping from prison, the mad Dr. Gustav Niemann and his loyal hunchbacked assistant, Daniel, revive not only Count Dracula, but the Wolf Man and Frankenstein's Monster as well, all in order to exact revenge upon Niemann's many enemies.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: An American who sells cars in England receives a mysterious invitation from an old, eccentric millionaire to visit his house in which he lives with his twin brother.

programme.title: House of Wax
programme.desc: A sculptor opens a wax museum to showcase the likenesses of famous historical figures, but quickly runs into trouble when his business partner demands the exhibits become more extreme in order to increase profits.

programme.title: I Bury the Living
programme.desc: Through a series of macabre "coincidences," the newly-elected director of a cemetery (Richard Boone) begins to believe that he can cause the deaths of living owners of burial plots by merely changing the push-pin color from white (living) to black (dead) on a large wall map of the cemetery that notes those plots.

programme.title: Invaders from Mars
programme.desc: A young boy learns that space aliens are taking over the minds of earthlings.

programme.title: Invisible Agent
programme.desc: The Invisible Man's grandson uses his secret formula to spy on Nazi Germany.

programme.title: Island of Terror
programme.desc: A small, isolated island community's terrorised from a horde of creeping, blobbish, tentacled monsters which liquefy and digest the bones from living creatures. The community struggles to fight back, against these horrors.

programme.title: Lady Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Frankenstein's daughter and his assistant/her lover continue his experiments in an attempt to rebuild his legacy after he is killed by his psychotic, murderous first monster.

programme.title: Night of the Living Dead
programme.desc: A ragtag group of Pennsylvanians barricade themselves in an old farmhouse to remain safe from a horde of flesh-eating ghouls that are ravaging the Northeast of the United States.

programme.title: Oingo Boingo Live At The Ritz (1985)
John Lennon and the plastic ono band Toronto 1969
Van Halen - Live Without a Net (1986)
Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers - Rockpalast (1977)
Jethro Tull - Jack In The Green
World Championship Wrestling - 3x33 - World Championship Wrestling 118
World Championship Wrestling - 3x34 - World Championship Wrestling 119
World Championship Wrestling - 3x35 - World Championship Wrestling 120
World Championship Wrestling - 3x36 - World Championship Wrestling 121
World Championship Wrestling - 3x37 - World Championship Wrestling 122
World Championship Wrestling - 3x38 - World Championship Wrestling 123
World Championship Wrestling - 3x39 - World Championship Wrestling 124
World Championship Wrestling - 3x40 - World Championship Wrestling 125
World Championship Wrestling - 3x41 - World Championship Wrestling 126
World Championship Wrestling - 3x42 - World Championship Wrestling 127
World Championship Wrestling - 3x43 - World Championship Wrestling 128
World Championship Wrestling - 3x44 - World Championship Wrestling 129
World Championship Wrestling - 3x45 - World Championship Wrestling 130
World Championship Wrestling - 3x46 - World Championship Wrestling 131
World Championship Wrestling - 3x47 - World Championship Wrestling 132
World Championship Wrestling - 3x48 - World Championship Wrestling 133
World Championship Wrestling - 3x49 - World Championship Wrestling 134
World Championship Wrestling - 3x50 - World Championship Wrestling 135
Columbo
programme.desc: A popular country western singer wants to get rid of his religiously zealous wife who's been using blackmail to control every aspect of his life. But a murder made to look like an airplane accident does not fool the wily Lt. Columbo.

programme.title: Maude
programme.sub-title: Maude's Problem (a.k.a.) Maude and the Psychiatrist
programme.desc: Carol's odd behavior leads Maude to believe she's having an affair. When Maude learns Carol is really seeing a psychiatrist, she is determined to get to the bottom of the situation and find out why her daughter needs therapy. Maude promptly makes an enexpected visit to Carol's psychiatrist but soon finds herself being analyzed.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Carol and Maude thinks it's cute when they discover a curious Phillip playing doctor with Arthur's equally curious granddaughter. However, the prudish Arthur insists that Phillip be severely punished which leads to a debate between Maude, Cariol and Arthur over morals.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Rose is apprehensive about a visit from her daughter and granddaughter, since their reason for coming is to settle Rose's late-husband's will. Meanwhile, Blanche gets caught up in the tabloids of a society murder.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Blanche's visiting niece is a chip right off the old block when she dates a different man all night every night. Meanwhile, Dorothy does battle with a mouse living in the house.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy is hesitant about having foot surgery just as she was scheduled to tap dance with Blanche and Rose at a dance recital.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: A lawyer comes to the hotel saying he represents, Sophia'a former son-in-law, Stan and he tells them that he died and that Stan's final wish is for them to be at his service. Later when Sophia's alone, Stan shows up and tells her that he's about to face criminal charges so to avoid them, he faked his death and will be going away.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred's cousin arrives with his new wife and his not-so-attractive stepdaughter, Betty Jean. When Fred finds out that she will inherit $10,000 if she marries, he tries to fix it so that Lamont will marry her. However, Fred's plan backfires when Lamont catches on to his scheme.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Gloria suspects handy-panky when she meets the beautiful blonde Mike's been tutoring after school hours,

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: If it's a major holiday and Archie and Mike are sitting at the table, it's sure to mean one thing: verbal fireworks. This time, the holiday is Thanksgiving and the topic is the religion of the Stivics' unborn baby. Archie wants to raise his grandchild as a Christian; Mike suggests that the child will make the decision when he or she is old enough.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie gets a lesson in civil liberties when he's arrested using outlawed tear gas to protect himself against a mugger.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Cynthia & Gilda vs. Ava & Wanda vs. Shannon & Angie

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Bobbie & Ellarye vs. Tracie & Joann vs. Doreen & Jeanette

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are auto mechanics who need money so they can marry their girls. When some escaped convicts pull into their garage, the boys manage to capture them and use the reward money to marry their sweethearts.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Joe is engaged but can't get married until he recovers the engagement ring which has disappeared. The stooges suspect the ring was stolen by Elmo, a beefy bully, who works at the same factory they do. They confront Elmo in the company gym, but he's too tough for them. Fortunately Joe's girl is even tougher, and she gets Elmo to confess and return the ring.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are pest exterminators who drum up business by planting vermin in a ritzy mansion where a party is going on. The boys are hired, but must dress as guests to work unobserved. They disrupt the party and a wild pie fight ensues.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are castaways from a garbage scow who land on Dead Man's Island where everyone is living in olden times. To escape from the governor, they disguise Curly as a Maharaja and win permission to journey to their own country to fetch presents. The governor is fooled, but the boys run into more trouble in the den of Black Louie the pirate where Curly is forced into a knife throwing contest with Larry as the target. Things look bad until a mis-thrown knife cuts the rope that holds the chandelier and it crashes down on Black Louie's men. With the pirates defeated, Moe decides to take over as ruler of the island.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Joe dreams that the stooge's sister Birdie has died and been reincarnated as a horse. The stooges take Birdie home but must conceal her from the snoopy landlord. They succeed, but more complications ensue when Birdie gives birth to a colt. Joe wakes up to suffer some abuse from the real Birdie (Moe in drag), when he tells her he dreamed she was a horse.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are carpenters who are re-staining some furniture they've delivered to a boarding house. The plot gets complicated when the boys confront some crooks who are hiding out there. They defeat the bad guys with the help of the varnished furniture which sticks the head crook to a chair.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: A professor attempts to win a bet by turning the stooges into gentlemen. After some lessons in etiquette, the boys make their society debut at a fancy party. They soon revert to their old habits and a wild pie fight ensues.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc: Svengoolie presents 'The Brides of Dracula'. Vampire hunter Van Helsing returns to Transylvania to destroy handsome bloodsucker Baron Meinster, who has designs on beautiful young schoolteacher Marianne.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The power company cuts off the Conners' power. Dan and Roseanne must look at Darlene in a new light after David spends the night in her room.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Jackie are stunned when their mother announces she is moving to Lanford; Jackie agonizes over dating Fisher, a perfect, but much, much younger man.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne mortifies Darlene with a sweet sixteen party, then sends her off to her friends while the adults enjoy the festivities, which include a game of Truth or Dare; the Tildens move in next door; Darlene dyes her hair black to make Roseanne mad, but is shocked when Roseanne reacts differently.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: Looking for Loans in All the Wrong Places
programme.desc: Denied a loan to open the restaurant, Roseanne must turn to her mother; Darlene catches D.J. peeping at neighbor Molly.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne sends everyone to a party while she stays home -- and is visited by the ghosts of Halloween past, present, and future.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Nancy takes Roseanne and Jackie by surprise with the announcement that she and Marla are gay lovers; Bev announces she is moving into a Lanford retirement center, which leaves Roseanne feeling over the hill.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The Lunch Box welcomes its first customers, including a weirdo whose presence sends the gals to a self-defense class. Meanwhile, Arnie questions his own manhood after hearing that Nancy is gay.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne reluctantly allows Darlene to go to a rock concert in nearby Rockford with Molly -- who abandons her.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Dummy displays have been made of Mrs. Slocombe and Mr. Humphries, and Mr. Grace is keen on expanding the advertisement for Grace Brothers further. He thinks that the store should have ordinary people (the staff) in a commercial advertising the various types of clothes on sale. With Mr. Humphries directing, and the rest of the staff as characters in the script, they set out to make the commercial.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The Royal Family is rumored to be stopping by Grace Brothers for a visit, so the staff prepare for it with decorations and fancy greetings. Meanwhile Mr. Tebbs is established as a new reoccurring character.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
In another "Fan-Tastic Friday," Bill and Toony take requests and answer mail from viewers.
Featured cartoons: Hare Do (1949) Dixieland Droopy (1954) Porky's Hotel (1939) Super Pink (1966) Beep, Beep (1952)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: A police commissioner provides a false alibi for a neighbor who killed his wife in a fit of rage. He then kills his own wife in cold blood and blackmails the neighbor into faking an alibi for himself. Lt. Columbo has to untangle this mess.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline host a grand opening celebration at their new cupcake shop, and Max invites her former flames to show off her success.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max sees Sophie depressed after being swindled by the contractor in charge of building her dream house, she offers to make her a silent partner in the cupcake business, but Caroline and Sophie have very different visions for the company.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan bring this special edition of Prime Time Wrestling to the Trump Plaza Hotel to preview all the action and excitement set to take place at WrestleMania V. Plus, The Rockers' Shawn Michaels and Marty Jannetty take on The Brain Busters' Arn Anderson and Tully Blanchard in a featured Tag Team Match.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: One way, or another, Linda's sister, Gayle, is gonna find Bob at Dr. Yin's dental office and she's gonna get him, get him, get him, get him... One day, maybe next week, on a a ski trip. Tina tries Persuasia on Dr. Yin, but settles for friends with dental benefits. Gene and Louise challenge each other for days over a jawbreaker; and Dr. Yin goes all "Marathon Man" on a perceived rival.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After Frank stops by Ray and Debra's house to borrow some ointment for his thighs, he becomes unusually interested in spending time with Ray. Initially, Ray finds it weird and is concerned that his father has suffered a physical trauma to the head, but after two of Frank's friends from the lodge stop by, all becomes clear.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra are discouraged when they realize that their sex life isn't what it used to be. They reassuring each other that it's not an unusual occurrence for older couples to experience since the kids are always underfoot, they're sleep deprived, etc. Trying to make themselves feel better, they approach Frank and Marie asking them what constitutes a "normal" sex life for an older couple.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: After lying to Carrie, Deacon and Doug go to Atlantic City where Doug loses a lot of money, while Deacon makes a lot of it. Deacon used money that he was holding for Doug to win the money, so Doug wants some of it. Carrie takes Arthur to a musical.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: After Deacon tells him he's a Big Brother, Doug feels bad because he doesn't give anything back to society and decides to become a Big Brother as well. Unfortunately for Doug, the boy that's assigned to him doesn't seem to be very enthusiastic, to say the least. Meanwhile, Carrie accidentally says 'I love you' when she's on the phone with Doug Pruzan, one of her bosses.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George dates a girl from Elaine's art class and wonders whether or not her humorous caricature of him is a sign that she likes him or not. Thanks to Newman's fleas, Jerry's visiting parents are forced to stay in Elaine's luxury hotel room. Elaine gets a potential job offer but accidentally leaves the manuscript in Jerry's flea-infested apartment.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: A booking agent is overly attentive to Jerry; Pitt puts Elaine in his will; Kramer starts gambling again.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan snub the neighborhood association by putting up gaudy Xmas decorations, and become furious with Becky when she gives the check they have given her for college to Mark and takes a job as a waitress in a tacky bar.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne joins a women's club in hopes of establishing useful contacts, leaving D.J. high and dry with a weird classmate whose mother also belongs. Meanwhile, Mark drops a bombshell on Becky when Dan discovers that he has dropped out of school.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Toby leaves Dunder Mifflin, and Michael plans a huge goodbye party... out of joy. Angela refuses to help with it, so Michael turns to Phyllis. Dwight and Meredith harass Holly, their new HR representative.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: A documentary crew arrives at the offices of Dunder Mifflin to observe the employees and learn about modern management. Manager Michael Scott tries to paint a happy picture, while sales rep Jim fights with his nemesis Dwight and flirts with receptionist Pam.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Marge leaves the house to recuperate from a skating injury, everything goes to hell - until Lisa convinces Homer and Bart they have leprosy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer gets a bucket stuck on his head, and when it is removed the town is convinced that Bart has the powers of a faith healer. Then mobster Fat Tony tries to turn the boy's spiritual abilities into more sinister directions.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Mr. Burns has to go the Mayo Clinic in Minnesota for a checkup, he chooses The Simpsons to take care of his mansion while he is gone. Homer and his friends take Mr. Burns yacht into international waters, where they end up being targeted by modern day pirates.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons get an abused horse and make him a contender in horse racing. Homer finds out a secret conspiracy about jockeys. Lisa tries to reach President Clinton when the results of a school band talent competition seem tainted.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer's juvenile antics at the speedway track result in Maude Flanders falling to her death. For once Homer is genuinely sorry for his bereaved neighbour and attempts to help Ned find a new lady-friend via video dating. However, it does not work and Ned is in danger of losing his faith - until he meets the lead singer in a Christian rock band, Rachel Jordan, with whom he strikes up a new friendship.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: A man offers the Bundy's $10,000 if Buck will breed with his champion dog, Lady Bird. So, Al has to coach Buck into knowing the varieties of breeding with other dogs the proper way. But needless to say it goes wrong and Al is stuck with the bill. Meanwhile, Kelly becomes Bud's fashion counselor and dresses him up in medieval costumes.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Marcy and Jefferson announce that they are expecting a baby, sending Al into pearls of laughter saying that it's the end of Jefferson's life, until Peggy announces the same. Al begins to lose his hair at an accelerated rate, thinking it's the end of the world for him. Kelly and Bud aren't pleased with the news either and take it out on Al.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Peggy and Marcy's constant demands for belly rubbing and expensive baby accessories drive Al crazy, prompting him to make a run for it. He and Jefferson end up at a bar connected to the bus station and watch semi-nude dancing girls while talking about a new and better life without their wives.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When Al unearths his father's old hammer, he decides to turn the garage into his own private room to escape his pregnant wife. All goes well, until Jefferson and the guys crash in. Even worse, it's only a matter of time before their own pregnant spouses find out.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Kelly earns a quick $1,000 at hustling pool to buy a gown for the local Miss Cheese competition, and a suspicious Al is out for blood, mistakenly thinking that Kelly is into prostitution. Al is then out of blood when he sells his to get Kelly and manager Jefferson the money for a major pool game bet.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: Looking for a Desk in All the Wrong Places
programme.desc: While Peggy is away helping Marcy recover her precious childhood belongings that Jefferson abandoned in a storage locker to be auctioned off in which he pocked and gambled away the money, Al, Kelly, and Bud hold an anti-baby meeting and makes plans for a new "Bundy World Order" that includes food, clean clothes and a huge hooter-ed oriental maid to replace the "pegzilla" Peggy.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Peggy gets jealous of Buck when the family pay him all the attention, and she flees home to W* County.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: If I Could See Me Now
programme.desc: Bud and Kelly convince Al to get glasses after a few minor accidents.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Saturday night sees Fred heading over to Donna's for a date that he didn't tell her about before hand. Once he arrives, he discovers Donna already has a date with a man who's won the decathlon every year in the Senior Olympics. Wanting to outdo him, Fred decides to compete in the Olympics this year and beat his romantic competition.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred is a Scrooge-like miser during the Christmas season and his friends try to get him to join into the Christmas spirit, but he rejects the attempts. After being accused of a being a Scrooge, Fred falls asleep and dreams up "A Christmas Carol." Now it is up to the spirits to take on the challenge that his friends failed at.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred lands in the hospital after an accident in which Fred accidentally witnesses a murder attempt. With a guarded hospital room, Fred becomes suspicious of everyone since the real criminal wants him killed. However, Fred is after the reward money he'll get if he testifies despite putting his life on the line.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont has been saving for acting school and has just enough money. Meanwhile, Fred sees a commercial for a get rich quick scheme and decides to give it a try. Thus, he invests Lamont's money in a supply of Whopper Choppers that he must try to sell. However, selling them is the hard part.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris must deal with the fact he is ready to undergo ""major"" surgery and he begins by trying to make peace with Sharon.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: When his pen-pal gets released from prison, she comes to stay with Chris. She takes him hostage when the police come after her when she starts criminal activities under Chris' nose.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs' peace and quiet is interrupted when Willoughby, a dog, digs Bugs out of his rabbit hole. Willoughby takes an immediate disliking to Bugs, and he starts to chase him into a lake.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A small airplane takes its passengers on a world tour.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: An spot-gag view of sports.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: The two curious dogs attempt to retrieve a bone from a frozen river.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A construction foreman conducts like a symphony orchestra as they build a skyscraper.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Elmer expects to find rest and relaxation at Jellostone National Park, but he mistakenly sets camp in the neighborhood of Bugs' rabbit hole, and Bugs (and a neighboring bear) don't have much leisure in mind.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A tour that takes us to different places around the world that includes: The African Jungle, The Carribean, The Swiss Alps, Veronica Lake, and The Pyramids.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.sub-title: The Wabbit Who Came to Supper
programme.desc: Elmer Fudd is already hunting Bugs when he learns that he will inherit three million dollars from Uncle Louie if he doesn't harm animals, especially rabbits.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys drive Principal McVicker insane, sending him to a mental home.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Burger World starts a new delivery service; the boys make the first -- and last -- run.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
A blackout forces Beavis and Butt-head out of the house to find a place to watch TV.
videos:
Radiohead - "Creep"
Morrissey - "November Spawned a Monster"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head plan to fake getting injured so they can get insurance money; real injuries follow.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys go skeet shooting and Butt-head downs a jumbo jet.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head both want to wear the Burger World giant hamburger costume. Middle-aged Beavis and Butt-Head get a warehouse job at the world's biggest internet retail company. You know the one we mean.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head are invited to sleep over at Cody's house when his parents mistakenly think they're good friends.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head are almost recruited into the Army by Sergeant Dick Leaky, a.k.a. "The Rooster."

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After watching ""The Beverly Hillbillies"" on TV, Beavis and Butt-head decide to dig for oil but break a sewage line instead.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After failing math in Mr. Buzzcut's class, the boys get sent back to 7th grade, but they don't do so well there either, and keep getting sent back -- all the way to kindergarten.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Middle-aged Beavis and Butt-Head run out of beer money, so Beavis has to find a job.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys find a wounded baby bird and try to ""take care"" of it.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Get out your pencils and crayons, 'cause the gang at Pee Wee's Playhouse is sketching Pee Wee's favorite subject -- Pee Wee! The Playhouse Gang joins in the fun when Pee Wee-o assigns everyone a secret name-o. But then Pee Wee develops a severe case of shrunken head when Jambi the Genie's magic helps him out of the mouse hole -- and the entire gang wonders if he'll ever be "okay".

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: It's party time at the Playhouse, and you're invited! The whole gang is here: Cowboy Curtis, Captain Carl, Reba the Mail Lady, and the lovely Miss Yvonne! You'll want to join in the Hokey-Pokey, scream when you hear the secret word ("this"), and play Pin-the-Tail-on-the-Conky!

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Desperate for a way to pay their overdue rent, Max and Caroline get involved with a group of sketchy customers willing to pay top dollar for their cupcakes. Recurring guest star RYAN HANSEN appears as Andy.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony continues to haggle with Johnny Sack and the two Carmines over the Esplanade profits. Carmela makes a decorating date she can't keep; Furio proves a reluctant companion to Tony on a casino junket; Paulie plays an impatient chaperone to some ladies from the nursing home; and Meadow debates the true meaning of a literary classic with her mom.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: When the Neighborhood experiences a small glitch, Michael has to resolve the issue with Janet before it gets out of control.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Janet creates a big problem for Michael. Meanwhile, Eleanor lets Chidi in on a secret, and Jason puts Tahani in a difficult position.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael receives a surprise visitor. Meanwhile, Eleanor, Chidi, Tahani and Jason attempt to solve a riddle.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: While Rhaenyra struggles to come to terms with her son's murder, in King's Landing, Alicent grows concerned that Aegon's Small Council may lead them to an all-out war. Larys suggests Aegon needs a new Hand, and Rhaenyra arrives at a fateful decision.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Lord Stark and his daughters arrive at King's Landing to discover the intrigues of the king's realm.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Eddard investigates Jon Arryn's murder. Jon befriends Samwell Tarly, a coward who has come to join the Night's Watch.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: House takes the case of a young girl who has been diagnosed with pancreatitis. When he takes her divorced parents to court to force them to allow treatment because they can't agree, the judge awards guardianship of the girl to Cuddy. Meanwhile, Tritter continues his strategy to force House to admit to his drug use by offering one of the team members a deal.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Wilson arranges for House to make a deal with Tritter, but House refuses. Meanwhile, Cuddy cuts House off Vicodin and removes him from the team's case: a 15-year-old little person who entered the hospital with a collapsed lung and anemia.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Moe sees that Homer can absorb massive blows to the head, he turns him into a prizefighter and sets up a match with heavyweight champion Drederick Tatum.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons give a lift to a man called Larry who is hitching to Springfield after discovering that he is Burns's long-lost son. Burns is initially happy to see him but tires of Larry's uncouth slobbishness and Larry is befriended by Homer. He decides that Larry might win back his father's love by means of a fake kidnapping. The plan is exposed but Burns forgives Larry, who then goes home to his wife and children.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Whilst Marge and Lisa are at the coast helping to clean up after an oil spill, Bart, left to his own devices, comes upon a house of ill repute and soon ends up working on the door. Marge is appalled to find that Homer knew but did nothing to stop his son and leads a deputation of local citizens demanding its closure, culminating in her accidentally bull-dozing it.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: At a party at the Simpsons' house, Milhouse's parents Luann and Kirk publicly row and later split up, Kirk having to go and live at a motel while Luann takes up with younger Gladiator Chase. Fearing that his own marriage is in jeopardy, Homer divorces Marge so that he can remarry her properly and with ceremony in front of all their friends. Kirk attempts the same with Luann but fails.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa develops a crush on Nelson and tries to change him into a better person. Meanwhile, Homer finds an autodialer and starts a telemarketing scam.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A hurricane destroys the Flanders' home and they have to move into the church basement. The people of Springfield gather to rebuild their house, but when Ned sees the poor workmanship, he has a complete breakdown.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: El Viaje Misterioso de Nuestro Jomer (The Mysterious Voyage of Homer)
programme.desc: After eating a dish laced with potent peppers during the chili cookoff, Homer has hallucinations where a mystical coyote urges him to find his soulmate.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer witnesses something out of this world in Springfield woods, but no one believes him -- not even FBI agents Fox Mulder and Dana Scully, who come to investigate the incident.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Twisted World of Marge Simpson
programme.desc: Marge goes to work for Frank Ormond,making and selling pretzels and,when he dies,sets up in business for herself. Initially sales are poor until Homer brings in local Mafia boss Fat Tony as her business partner. Sales boom as a result but,unfortunately,any profits vanish due to Fat Tony's 'business agreement' with Homer.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Burns takes his staff to Mount Useful for a team-building exercise,the object being to reach a cabin at the top. Paired with Homer,Burns cheats and they reach their destination but experience cabin fever when snowed in by an avalanche. Eventually Homer uses a propane stove to turn the cabin into a sled that sends them back to safety.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: WWE Champion "Stone Cold" Steve Austin vows to get his hands on The Undertaker. The rivalry between DX and The Nation takes a violent turn as both groups face off in a Street Fight. Mrs. Yamaguchi-San looks to get even with Val Venis. Plus, Gangrel makes his Raw debut, Sable takes on Jacqueline in an Arm Wrestling Match, and more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny finds a potential buyer for the town, while David and Alexis discover they may have become more attached to Schitt’s Creek than they ever thought possible.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: The Roses search for their missing son, David, while Alexis takes her relationship with Mutt to the next level despite technically being engaged to Ted.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline accidentally loses Han’s expensive fish while cleaning the tank, Max considers selling a sentimental gift she just received from her mother in order to pay for a new one.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline takes an abandoned bicycle in order to start a delivery service for their cupcake business, but discovers that Max never learned how to ride one.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's partnership - and friendship - is almost torn apart when Caroline drags Max to a seminar to help brand their cupcake business.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline put their apartment up for rent on Airbnb, claiming it offers an authentic "Williamsburg experience," a group of supermodels decide it would be a fun place for a girls' weekend.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline get a bank loan so they can mass-produce their cupcake T-shirts to sell at high-end boutiques. Also, the diner gang poses in Chestnut’s barn as part of a holiday decorating contest Sophie entered.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline select a factory to produce their cupcake T-shirts, but become suspicious of the work conditions there due to their overly-enthusiastic employees.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Past And The Furious
programme.desc: Caroline is shocked when she receives a Lamborghini for her birthday, which her father ordered for her four years ago. Meanwhile, Oleg has something big planned for Sophie.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max, Caroline, Earl and Han help Oleg move into Sophie’s apartment until she begins turning away all of his things.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are arrested for breaking into a cute guy’s apartment to recover the rings Caroline left behind after she spent the night.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline get into a legal battle with two prep school teens who knocked off their cupcake T-shirts and are selling them.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline find out that one of their customers at the diner is actually a successful photographer who has been keeping a secret.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Sophie’s wedding shower is interrupted by a hostage situation and Max and Caroline’s cupcake business gets unwanted attention when a tenant is arrested while wearing their company’s T-shirt.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: A handsome businessman returns Max and Caroline’s missing cat, Nancy, who comes home with a big surprise.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to bring in extra money to help pay off their bank loan by taking second jobs as a pastry chef and a hostess at a chic restaurant.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max pursues Nashit, a handsome new waiter at The High, despite Joe’s “no hook-up” rule.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: With things turning around for Richmond, it's time for everyone to work on their issues—like Ted's discomfort, Nate's confidence, and Roy's attention.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Ted and Dr. Sharon realize they might have to meet each other halfway. Tensions are high as the team prepares for the semifinal.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: How the nervous system registers pain; astronomer Maria Mitchell.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Hydraulic principles; crystals; dinosaur descendants.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Electric-motor workings; how cultures measure time.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: The difference between frogs and toads; how plastics are made.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Lester learns the value of a dollar when Beakman tries to make "cents" out of currency systems; and Liza soaks up some information about water power.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: In Bill Nye the Science Guy: Forests, Nye shows students the levels of a forest, which include the canopy, the under story, and the floor. His special guest is Nalini Nadkarni, who has no qualms about going high up in the canopy to check out the wildlife and other happenings there

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: In this program, Bill points out the different ways in which humans and animals exchange information. He also talks about the ability of humans to store data in computers, books, and on videotapes.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball tries desperately to find out Darwin's secret.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Mr. Small teaches Gumball and Darwin various ways of becoming more honest.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin goes to an institute for geniuses.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Richard believes there's a ghost in the attic but is actually Mr. Robinson who Gumball moved up there after entering a catatonic state due to being kicked out by his wife. Gumball and Darwin work on trying to restore Mr. Robinson whilst Richard, unaware of this, sets traps for the ghost.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin want to become adults, Richard wants to lose weight. After accidentally having their cereal switched with supplements Richard ordered, Gumball, Darwin and Anais find themselves having very unusual growth spurts.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball learns how to date, so he can go out on a date with Penny. Later on it turns out that he is going to a funeral, not a date.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Gumball turns down an offer to join the Rejects Club, they seek to enact revenge against him.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The guys share the power of magic with Dad.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Ms. Simian cozies up to Gumball in order to win an award.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin are eager enough to do numerous chores at Mr. Robinson's house, until they accidentally destroy his car.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin is determined to continue his streak of stopping 52 crimes during crime season.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: The Inner Beauty of a Cactus
programme.desc: The Titans play Spin The Bottle as Starfire tries to improve her vocabulary through kissing.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans have a hard time deciding who gets to choose which movie they watch on movie night.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy writes a song for Raven to express his feelings, then begins touring with the song, which upsets Raven.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy writes a song for Raven to express his feelings, then begins touring with the song, which upsets Raven.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: It's back to school for the Titans as they teach each other about science and grammar.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans are determined to find out what happened to the missing 2'oclock hour during Daylight Saving Time.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: It's St. Patrick's Day and after wishing to become a leprechaun, Beast Boy becomes obsessed with gold.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: In a deranged tribute to the "I Love Lucy" show, Stimpy lavishes affection on a store-bought oven-ready chicken. Next, Powdered-Toast Man and Waffle-Woman face off in a fight-to-the-death; the world hangs in the balance.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.sub-title: Powdered Toast Man vs. Waffle Woman
programme.desc: A woman tells the story of how her waffle business was ruined because of Powdered Toast. Meanwhile Powdered Toast Man tries to grant a wish for Little Jimmy.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren & Stimpy are about to take that last long walk at the dog pound when a sweet rich old lady falls for them and adopts them.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: In this parody of Pinocchio, Renwaldo, the village of Egg Smithëe, makes a puppet boy name Egg Yölkeo.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren can't stand Stimpy anymore, so he sends Stimpy on a one-way ticket out of town. Then they are hit by a bus and get united (literally) in a cheap hospital.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head learn about evolution and dream of scoring as cavemen.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Over the course of a few hours, the boys attempt to master the subtle techniques of the game...in broad daylight.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head get to see the film the rest of us guys never got to see in school--for educational reasons only, of course.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys find a wounded baby bird and try to ""take care"" of it.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head sit in with a group for alcoholics to try to get some beer.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to a lingerie store and feel up the merchandise.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys try to find their own treatment for a nasty case of head lice.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys force Stewart to access porn sites on the internet from school.

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Young Ones (Ed & Stephanie) vs. Turkeys (Joe & Michelle)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc: The main plot for this episode is unknown, but it featured a Panasonic radio as a prize for one of the phone-in competitions, as most of the episodes from this 1979 season. This episode also featured a segment on baton twirling. The episode contains the well-known scene where Ross (before a major costume revamp) teaches Jono Gebert how to do a weather forecast on an actual CJOH news/weather set.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc: Tim constantly tries to get a date with Cindy, but keeps looking like a fool, losing her to Jim and David. To add insult to injury, he keeps being forced into wearing a dressâ€¦and he can't seem to find his pants, forcing him to go home wearing a bright, pink dress.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc: The main plot for this episode is unknown, but it featured kids' jokes and opinions filmed at the Winterloo slide at Ottawa's N.A.C.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Howard Stern's radio regulars go toe to toe against Hollywood rehab addicts and alkies...it's the classic battle of the wack pack vs. the crack pack. Games - Rotating Surfboards of Death, The Crate Escape, Sperm Whalers, and Sinkers and Floaters.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's 'Roid Rage' as the pumped up blood-doping Pro Athletes take on the Fashion industry's Super Models...it's the classic battle of Anabolic Steroids versus Anorexic mind voids! Games - Eat Shitake, Spit Ball, Irritable Bowl Syndrome, and Log Drop.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Justice vs. TV Motor Sports Shows
programme.desc: It's Hollywood infamy and mechanical reverie as Celebrity Justice takes on TV's Motor shows. It's the classic battle of Fallen Stars and Souped-up Cars. Games - Sack Lunch, Wall Buggers, Little Man in the Boat, and Brass Balls.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's a cinematic bonanza as the big screen Tough Guys go head-to-head with the box office Chick Flicks...it's the classic battle of Rambo versus Romance. Games - Slippery Slope of Slanted Death, Tumbling Dominoes of Doom, Catch Hell, and Log Drop.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: The World of Hip Hop vs. Hollywood Horror Movies
programme.desc: It's music and monsters as the world of Hip Hop takes on Hollywood Horror Movies. It's the classic battle of Rappers vs. Rippers. Games - Cheeze Squeezers, Runaway Stump, Dash to Death, and Sinkers and Floaters.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Mega-Millionaires vs. Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: It's have-mores versus has-beens as the mega-millionaires take on the Where are they Nows? It's the classic battle of Gazillions vs. Gilligans. Games - Great Holes of Glory, Mud Butlers, Ball Busters, and Rotating Surfboards of Death.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Today, it's oil slicks and bad flicks as NASCAR takes on Hollywood's Box Office Bombs. It's the classic battle of fast cars vs. 'Ishtars.' Games - Drop Dead Line, Muddy Runs, Chum in the Mouth, and Battlefeltch Earth.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's vaporizers and exercisers as celebrated Potheads take on the Fitness Industry...it's the classic battle of degenerate Stoners versus strong Boners. Games - Bagel bumpers, Wallbangers, Circle Jerkers & Brass Balls.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The threat of a Lawndale hurricane causes people to start singing and dancing!

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: An obstruction by her glasses during a driving lesson causes Daria to contemplate trading them in for contact lenses. The trouble is, she fears that by doing so she will be viewed as being just as vain and superficial as the rest of her vapid fellow classmates.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The new spirit of "volunteerism" is mandatory at Lawndale High. While reading to senior citizens, Daria finds that her unenthusiastic tone makes her just as unpopular at the nursing home as at school.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A fantasy show wherein Daria and Jane have to convince Christmas, Halloween and Guy Fawkes Day to return to Holiday Island.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn gets roped into organizing the school dance; she fobs the job off on Jane.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The publisher of a "Savvy"-like teen magazine comes to Lawndale to hang out with Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria is forced to work side by side with Kevin at a mall nut stand.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: All the prodigal Lanes come home at once, forcing Jane to temporarily move in with Daria.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A new experimental enzyme being tested at the Wonder White Sub company laboratory bonds with an aggressive molecule and escapes down the drain. It starts attacking people, pinching underwear and tries to turn everything white.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold end up in a time-traveling clock when they investigate an attempted robbery of the crown jewels.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: What a 3 Point Turn-Up For the Book
programme.desc: Danger Mouse goes after his car, which has taken off on its own, keeping Danger Mouse from attending an award ceremony.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold is instrumental in keeping alien, J.J. Quark, from taking over the Earth.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: GB, the Terrible Toad is using a giant reflector stashed somewhere in the alps to melt the ice caps of the Arctic. His plan is to flood the world and sell rubber dinghies and rubber ducks at inflated prices.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: With Greenback on vacation, Stiletto uses a ray to create giant spiders to take over London.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Radio Control Plane - Square On Balloon - Armadillo Patient - Folded Paper Flips - Vectrex Drawing - Oldest Living Thing - Touch - Secret Inks - Fireproof Money

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Plastic Hot Air Balloon - Glue Powered Bugs - Apple Flower - Binary Number System - Lung's Capacity - Unbreakable Container - Spoon Into Glass - Suspended Book
